nuggets.headline: A radio host first spotted Jamee Cornelia in a collaborator's Instagram reel
nuggets.text: The host of WEFT 90.1 FM's "The Midnight Drive" was already following Jamee Cornelia's visual art, but it was seeing her in a reel by fellow creative June Jizzel that prompted the interview. This moment of peer-to-peer amplification reflects a conscious shift for Cornelia, who has made it a goal to collaborate more after years of handling most creative duties herself.

source.title: The Midnight Drive with Damien Jaxxyn: Interview with Jamee ...
source.publisher: WEFT 90.1 FM, Community Radio for East Central Illinois
source.quoteSnippet: I originally like I saw you on my my my rock page... but apparently I I originally saw you in a in a June Jizzel like uh reel.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia, a multi-talented artist with roots in the Atlanta creative scene.

nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia's hip-hop is built on a four-year punk rock foundation
nuggets.text: Before establishing her solo career, Jamee Cornelia spent four years as the lead vocalist for the punk band Howling Star. During that time, she and her bandmates founded Punk Black, their own concert series. This background in Atlanta's punk scene provides the framework for the raw energy and genre-agnostic approach you hear in her music today.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.publisher: Voyage ATL Magazine | ATL City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: I was also a lead vocalist for a punk band called Howling Star for 4 years and we started our own concert series called Punk Black while I was also in a hip-hop collective called Visibly Inflight for my solo work.

nuggets.headline: The next step after Jamee Cornelia is Nashville's Namir Blade
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia's work is part of a larger movement of Southern artists redefining hip-hop with a focus on humanity and organic connection over posturing. Namir Blade operates on that same wavelength, creating from a similar multi-disciplinary, DIY ethos. Both artists repurpose nostalgia with a brutal effectiveness that feels distinctly post-2020. Start with Blade's album *Metropolis*.

source.title: Bandcamp Picks: Jamee Cornelia & Cecily Renns - MGRM
source.quoteSnippet: it’s a true post-COVID record. Which is to say, it’s a kind of blueprint for what might come next—a robust sense of humanity; a tendency for organic connection over needless posturing...

nuggets.imageCaption: Namir Blade, a key figure in Nashville's alternative hip-hop scene.

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia is a multi-disciplinary artist from Marietta, Georgia, whose work is informed by a deep history in punk rock, skate culture, and DIY video production. Her music channels the raw, direct energy of her past projects into an unflinching and personal take on alternative hip-hop. She is also a music video director and co-runs the indie label L7 Records.
